title: corylus avellana f. pendula
intro: Corylus avellana f. pendula, also known as the pendulous hazel, is a cultivar of the hazel tree, characterized by its weeping or pendulous branches. The leaves and nuts of this plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and fever, due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Corylus avellana f. pendula are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for medicinal use, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits.
